Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses it 366/2 = 183 times in a leap year. Multiplying the amount used each time by the number of uses gives 183 * 1/4 = 45.75 ounces of shampoo needed for the entire year. Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, she would need to divide the total amount by the bottle size: 45.75 / 14 ≈ 3.27. Therefore, she needs [number of bottles], rounding up to the nearest whole number. [number of bottles] is the minimum she needs to get through the year.

Brittany should ensure that she has enough shampoo to cover the entire leap year, so it's important to calculate accurately. If she buys [number of bottles], she will have more than enough for her needs. It's also a good idea to check the expiration date on the shampoo to ensure it is still usable. With proper planning, she can avoid running out of shampoo during the leap year.
